MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

A Petition for Transfer was filed on December 12, 2017 with a hearing date of January 29, 2018. Status on filing of petition to change venue is off calendar.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-14-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: M. Armon Cooper for ZB, N.A. (Former Trustee).

The Court stated that before it is the petition to settle the sixth and final account by Mr. Cooper's client. The Court noted that Mr. Cooper had responded to the issues with regard to the petition and asked if there was anything further that he wished to add. Mr. Cooper stated that he wished to point out that it was the final accounting and that the issues were unique to this particular situation. At the conclusion of Mr. Cooper's comments, the Court stated that it would allow the final account noting that "we are where we are". The Court then granted ZB, N.A.'s Accounting Petition Re: Accounting #6, Report, and Fees (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque. Matter Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-15-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: M. Armon Cooper for petitioner ZB, N.A.

The Court inquired regarding the investment of trust funds in investments not authorized under the Probate Code. Mr. Cooper responded that the investments were made in index funds which were publicly traded on the New York Stock Exchange, and are therefore included within the term securities as defined and authorized in Probate Code Section 2574(a)(c)(3). He did not object to reimbursement to the trust for the overpayment of $58.36 or for the CPA fees for the accounting. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque. (Not reported)

MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Attorney Armon Cooper to appear on March 13, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why the Third Account has not been filed. The status hearing was also continued to that date.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

The Court was advised by the Probate Examiner that Counsel left a voice message informing the Court that he was not available to attend the hearing. The Order to Appear will not be issued. The continued date on the status hearing remains the same.
